_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL House Miami-Dade HD-108..112 (Joseph, Gantt, Fabricio, Borrero, Rizo). State tier. Marquee FL roll calls verified against official FL Legislature House vote PDFs. TWO CURRENCY CATCHES: (1) Dotie Joseph (D, HD-108) is TERM-LIMITED and FILED TO RUN FOR GOVERNOR in the Aug 18 2026 Dem primary -> candidacy_status running_higher_office; on SB 300 she was Not Voting (not a recorded NAY), so life cells nulled rather than inferred. (2) Ashley Gantt (D, HD-109) is running for FL SENATE SD-34, not HD-109 reelection -> running_higher_office. Two Ds uniformly progressive (no conservative deviation). Three Rs (Fabricio HD-110, Borrero HD-111, Rizo HD-112) uniformly conservative, no liberal-side deviation; Rizo scored on 13 pdftotext-verified roll calls, Borrero on lead-authored abolition+pride-flag-ban+religious-liberty bills, Fabricio on SB 300 YEA + FRTL/NRA endorsements + HJR 583 co-sponsorship + LEO bills.

profile.education: BA Political Science (honors), Yale University; JD, Georgetown Law
profile.background: Born in Haiti, immigrated as a toddler, raised in North Miami. Civil-rights/government attorney; former federal judicial law clerk; former Interim City Attorney, North Miami Beach; President, Haitian Lawyers Association. In FL House since Nov 2018 (HD-108); House Minority Leader Pro Tempore (2022). Daughter of Pastor Gonel Joseph.
profile.confidence_note: Term-limited out of HD-108 in 2026; filed to run for GOVERNOR of Florida in the Aug 18, 2026 Democratic primary (committee 'Victory Now'). Office field kept as State Representative (still the sitting tier) pending the gubernatorial outcome.

dotie-joseph@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-108 (Miami-Dade/North Miami), Democrat. CURRENCY: term-limited after 8 years; FILED to run for GOVERNOR in the Aug 18, 2026 Democratic primary (a last-minute qualifying-week entry). Uniformly progressive record; no conservative cross-party deviation. HARD votes from official FL House roll-call PDFs: NAY HB 1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay'), NAY HB 543 (2023 permitless carry) plus filed multiple gun-restriction amendments ('pouring gasoline on an already steady-burning fire'). LED the Haitian-caucus fight AGAINST SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement), filing failed amendments. NOTE: on SB 300 (2023 6-week ban) she was NOT VOTING (not a recorded NAY) -> life cells left null, not inferred from party. No abortion-funding endorsement document, election, federalism, christian-liberty, or public-justice position located -> null.

4.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022) 'Don't Say Gay'/Parental Rights in Education (official House PDF: 'N Joseph-108') -- does not oppose LGBTQ content in schools.

2.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022), which restricts classroom instruction on sexual orientation/gender identity -- does not oppose SOGI curricula.
Led the Haitian-caucus fight against SB 1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/enforcement bill and filed failed amendments -- opposes immigration enforcement/anti-sanctuary preemption.

3.note: Opposed SB 1718 (2023), which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry (official House PDF: 'N Joseph-108').

1.note: As ranking subcommittee Democrat she filed multiple amendments to ADD gun restrictions/training requirements to HB 543, comparing relaxed gun law to 'pouring gasoline on to an already slow, steady-burning fire' -- supports gun control.

profile.education: BA English, University of Florida (2007); JD, Nova Southeastern University Shepard Broad College of Law (2016)
profile.background: Teach For America (Mississippi Delta); 6 years a Miami-Dade public-school teacher; former assistant public defender, Broward County; founder/managing partner, Gantt Legacy Law, P.A. In FL House since Nov 2022 (HD-109).
profile.confidence_note: Not running for HD-109 reelection -- launched a 2026 campaign for FL SENATE District 34 (to succeed Sen. Shevrin Jones), with the entire Senate Democratic Caucus and Mayor Levine Cava behind her. Office field kept as State Representative (the sitting seat) pending the SD-34 race.

ashley-gantt@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-109 (Miami-Dade), Democrat. CURRENCY: not seeking HD-109 reelection -- running for FL SENATE SD-34 in 2026. Consistent progressive; NO conservative deviation. NINE party-line NAY votes pulled from official FL House roll-call PDFs (all 'N Gantt-109'): SB 300 (2023 6-week ban), HB 289 (2026 fetal personhood), HB 1069 (2023 sex-ed/pronoun expansion), HB 1521 (2023 bathroom/biological-sex), HB 1 (2023 universal vouchers), HB 1205 (2025 ballot-initiative restrictions -- does not map to the election-integrity rubric cells -> nulled), SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement), HB 543 (2023 permitless carry), HB 759 (2025 gun-age rollback). Endorsed by Ruth's List Florida (abortion-rights PAC). Publicly challenged the 2024 school-chaplains bill (HB 931) on Establishment-Clause grounds. No federalism or public-justice conservative record located -> null.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (2023) 6-week ban and NAY on HB 289 (2026) fetal-personhood bill (both 'N Gantt-109') -- does not affirm personhood from conception in law.

1.note: Voted against even a 6-week restriction -- does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by Ruth's List Florida, an abortion-rights PAC.

2.note: Voted NAY on HB 1521 (2023) bathroom/biological-sex bill ('N Gantt-109') -- does not affirm immutable biological sex in law.

4.note: Voted NAY on HB 1069 (2023), the expansion of the Parental Rights in Education law restricting LGBTQ/gender-ideology instruction -- does not oppose LGBTQ content in schools.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers ('N Gantt-109'), warning it would 'jeopardize the ability for public schools to continue to be funded.'

2.note: Voted NAY on HB 1069 (2023), which restricts SOGI/sex-ed instruction -- does not oppose such curricula ('we need to inform them about their bodies').

3.note: Publicly challenged HB 931 (2024) school-chaplains bill on the House floor, raising proselytization/Establishment-Clause objections -- does not support prayer/chaplains in public institutions.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/enforcement bill ('N Gantt-109') and filed a failed amendment against it.

3.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry ('N Gantt-109').

1.note: Voted NAY on HB 759 (2025) gun-age rollback ('N Gantt-109') -- supports keeping the post-Parkland firearm-purchase-age restriction.

profile.education: BA world literatures & political science, Marymount Manhattan College (2005); JD, Nova Southeastern University Shepard Broad College of Law (2008)
profile.background: Cuban-American attorney; managing partner of The Fabricio Law Firm, P.A.; owns True Freedom Title, LLC. Former FL Third DCA law clerk; Broward County Charter Review Commission (2016-18). In FL House since Nov 2020 (HD-103, renumbered HD-110 in 2022); Vice Chair, Ways & Means.
profile.confidence_note: Re-elected UNOPPOSED for the 2026 cycle (no other candidate qualified for HD-110). Several 2023 marquee votes (HB 543, SB 1718, HB 1, marriage bills) could not be pulled to his individual roll-call line, so those cells are left null rather than inferred from the party-line tally.

tom-fabricio@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-110 (Miami-Dade -- Hialeah/Miami Lakes), Republican, re-elected unopposed for 2026. Uniformly conservative; no liberal-side deviation. HARD: voted YEA on SB 300 (2023 6-week ban) and ENDORSED by Florida Right to Life (2024). Co-sponsor of HJR 583 (2026) protecting religious expression / prayer in public schools. Strong pro-law-enforcement sponsorship slate (2025): co-sponsor HB 1371 (LEO protections), HB 383 (firearm rights for LEOs/servicemembers), HB 653 (capital-felony aggravating factors); prime sponsor HB 317 (complaints process). NRA-endorsed (2022). Individual roll-call lines on HB 543/SB 1718/HB 1/the 2023 marriage bills could not be independently certified -> those cells null (not inferred). No election-integrity or federalism record located -> null.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB 300 (2023) 6-week ban and is endorsed by Florida Right to Life (2024 General Election, listed '110 Tom Fabricio') -- affirms the sanctity of unborn life.
Co-sponsor of HJR 583 (2026) protecting students'/staff's religious-viewpoint expression in public schools -- affirms the right to profess faith in the schoolhouse.

3.note: HJR 583 mandates a daily moment of silence and allows prayer at high-school championship events -- supports prayer in public institutions.

0.note: Affirmative pro-law-enforcement advocacy: co-sponsor of HB 1371 (2025 LEO protections) and HB 383 (firearm rights for LEOs/correctional officers); prime sponsor of HB 317 (officer-complaint process).

2.note: Co-sponsor of HB 653 (2025) adding aggravating factors for capital felonies -- supports proportional punishment of grave crimes.

1.note: NRA-endorsed (2022) -- aligns against red-flag laws, magazine limits, and gun registries.

profile.education: BA Political Science/Economics, Florida International University (2012); MBA, FIU; JD, St. Thomas University School of Law
profile.background: Attorney; former Sweetwater grants manager and city commissioner; account executive, T&G Constructors. In FL House since 2020 (HD-105, redrawn to HD-111 in 2022); Vice Chair, Rules & Ethics; Republican Whip on Judiciary.
profile.confidence_note: Several marquee individual roll-call lines (HB 543, SB 1718, HB 7049 CBDC, HB 1) could not be certified, so those cells are scored only where a lead-authored bill, cosponsorship, endorsement, or on-record floor quote exists; the rest left null.

david-borrero@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-111 (Miami-Dade -- Doral/Sweetwater), Republican, running for reelection 2026, on the right flank of the caucus. Uniformly conservative; no liberal-side deviation. HARD (lead-authored bills + on-record quotes): LEAD AUTHOR of HB 1519 (2024) abortion-abolition/fetal-personhood bill ('a person exists from the moment of fertilization'), with SB 300 floor quotes calling abortion 'dismemberment of an innocent human child.' LEAD AUTHOR of the pride-flag ban (HB 901/HB 347) barring LGBTQ/political flags from government buildings and schools. LEAD AUTHOR of HB 835 (2026) protecting students'/personnel's religious-viewpoint expression in schools. COSPONSOR of HB 103 (2022) open/permitless carry; NRA-endorsed (2022). PREMISE CORRECTIONS confirmed by research: the gold/silver legal-tender bill is Rep. Bankson's HB 999, NOT Borrero's; HB 1205 ballot-initiative bill is Persons-Mulicka's. Individual CBDC/HB 543/SB 1718 roll-call lines unconfirmed -> null. No election-integrity or federalism record located -> null.

0.note: Lead author of HB 1519 (2024) declaring 'a person exists from the moment of fertilization'; stated 'an unborn child is a person' -- affirms personhood from conception.

1.note: Lead author of HB 1519 (2024), which bans nearly all abortions (abolition, not merely restriction); called abortion 'dismemberment of an innocent human child' on the SB 300 floor.

4.note: Lead author of the pride-flag ban (HB 901/HB 347) barring LGBTQ/political flags from government buildings and public schools -- opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy and schools.

0.note: Lead author of HB 835 (2026) prohibiting schools from discriminating against students/personnel for religious-viewpoint expression -- affirms the right to profess faith in public schools.

1.note: HB 835 protects students/personnel from academic penalty for religious-viewpoint expression -- supports conscience protection for Christian students and educators.

0.note: Cosponsor of HB 103 (2022) open + concealed permitless carry -- supports constitutional carry.

1.note: NRA-endorsed (2022) -- aligns against red-flag laws, magazine limits, and gun registries.

profile.education: BS Biological Sciences, Florida International University (1995); MS Educational Leadership & Administration, Nova Southeastern University (2000)
profile.background: Career educator -- teacher/coach at Barbara Goleman Senior High; administrator, Miami-Dade County Public Schools (1998-2006); education consultant / small-business owner. In FL House since Nov 2020 (HD-110, redrawn to HD-112 in 2022); Chair, Student Academic Success Subcommittee.

alex-rizo@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-112 (Miami-Dade -- Hialeah), Republican, running for reelection 2026. Uniformly conservative; no cross-party deviation. THIRTEEN roll calls verified directly from official FL House vote PDFs (pdftotext, 'Y Rizo-112'/'Y Rizo-110' pre-2022): HB 5 (2022 15-week ban) YEA, SB 300 (2023 6-week ban) YEA, HB 289 (2026 fetal personhood) YEA, HB 1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay') YEA, HB 1069 (2023 expansion) YEA, HB 1521 (2023 bathroom/biological-sex) YEA, HB 1 (2023 universal vouchers) YEA, HB 3 (2024 porn age-verification) YEA, SB 7054 (2023 anti-CBDC) YEA, HB 3 (2023 anti-ESG) YEA, HB 1 (2021 anti-riot) YEA, SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement) YEA, HB 543 (2023 permitless carry) YEA, HB 759 (2025 gun-age rollback) YEA. Former educator: co-introduced HB 1291 (2024) anti-CRT teacher-prep bill. HB 1205 (2025 ballot-initiative) YEA does not map to the election-integrity rubric cells -> nulled. No christian-liberty or federalism record located -> null.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB 289 (2026 fetal personhood/wrongful-death of an unborn child), SB 300 (2023 6-week ban), and HB 5 (2022 15-week ban) -- affirms personhood of the unborn.

2.note: Voted YEA on HB 1521 (2023) bathroom/biological-sex bill -- affirms immutable biological sex in public facilities.

4.note: Voted YEA on HB 1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay') and HB 1069 (2023 expansion) -- opposes LGBTQ-identity instruction in K-12 schools.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice voucher program.

2.note: Voted YEA on HB 1069 (2023) restricting SOGI/sex-ed instruction and co-introduced HB 1291 (2024) barring 'distorting' (CRT-flavored) teacher-prep curricula.

3.note: Voted YEA on HB 3 (2024) online protection of minors / pornography age-verification.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB 7054 (2023) excluding a Central Bank Digital Currency from the UCC definition of 'money' -- opposes CBDC.

4.note: Voted YEA on HB 3 (2023) anti-ESG 'Government and Corporate Activism' bill -- opposes ESG/corporate-activist capture.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB 1 (2021) 'Combating Public Disorder' anti-riot law and proposed a buffer-distance protection for police on duty -- affirmatively backs law enforcement.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB 1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/enforcement bill.

3.note: Voted YEA on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry and NAY on the weakening amendment.

1.note: Voted YEA on HB 759 (2025) rolling back the firearm-purchase age from 21 to 18 -- opposes the post-Parkland gun restriction.
